description: Define system-managed default contract domain model and invariants in PRD-aligned engineering notes.
Add persistent marker/identity strategy for default contracts (schema-level or deterministic identity rule).
Enforce uniqueness for default contract identity per tenant+client (constraint/index/upsert semantics).
Implement idempotent `ensureDefaultContractForClient` primitive in shared billing-domain layer.
Ensure primitive creates minimal valid rows (`contracts` + `client_contracts`) without requiring immediate service lines.
Set required contract defaults on ensure-created contract (status, currency, owner client, lifecycle-safe defaults).
Hook default-contract ensure into shared billing settings ensure/insert path.
Hook default-contract ensure into shared billing schedule ensure/update path.
Hook default-contract ensure into billing package cycle-anchor ensure path.
Hook default-contract ensure into billing package settings action insert/update path.
Hook default-contract ensure into billing credit-expiration settings insert/update path where billing settings are created.
Centralize duplicated billing-settings ensure logic so all callers share one default-contract ensure behavior.
Guard ensure hooks so null-setting override deletes do not unintentionally recreate default contracts unless policy requires.
Add optional fallback reconciliation hook for client-created events that bypass billing settings flows.
Audit and patch API generic client-delete path to invoke domain cleanup equivalent to package-level delete action.
Implement explicit cleanup for default-contract assignments on client deletion.
Define orphan default-contract cleanup policy for contract headers when assignments are removed.
Preserve non-destructive lifecycle for invoiced default contracts (soft lifecycle, no invalid hard-delete behavior).
Update scheduling resolver signature to accept `effectiveDate` for default contract-line determination.
Update billing resolver signature to accept `effectiveDate` for default contract-line determination.
Update resolver eligibility filters to use `start_date <= effectiveDate` and `end_date >= effectiveDate or null`.
Pass time-entry work date/start timestamp-derived date into scheduling save-path resolver.
Pass usage effective date into usage save/update resolver.
Align UI eligible-contract-lines endpoint/helper to use same effective-date-aware resolver logic.
Update time-entry UI caller to provide effective date to eligible-lines query.
Update usage UI caller to provide effective date to eligible-lines query.
Remove display-only default-selection behavior that differs from persisted assignment behavior.
Implement billing-engine reconciliation write-back for unresolved time rows with deterministic single eligible line.
Implement billing-engine reconciliation write-back for unresolved usage rows with deterministic single eligible line.
Guard reconciliation updates with null-check predicates to avoid overriding explicit assignments.
Ensure reconciliation logic is idempotent under repeated due-work runs.
Update unresolved due-work query semantics to exclude reconciled deterministic records.
Keep ambiguous/no-match records unresolved and visible for explicit handling.
Refactor automatic invoicing candidate builder to consume default-contract-aware unresolved model.
Refactor invoice generation selection-key parsing away from legacy non-contract key semantics in primary paths.
Maintain backward-safe parsing for historical keys only where migration compatibility is required.
Update recurring invoice run actions to use default-contract-aware selection data structures.
Add clear attribution metadata for grouped rows indicating explicit contract vs system-managed default contract source.
Add hard generation block when grouped row attribution metadata is missing and generation would be unsafe.
Update contract list row badges to show `System-managed default` label.
Add helper copy in contract list/detail: `Created automatically for uncontracted work`.
Restrict destructive/manual lifecycle actions for system-managed default contract rows in list/detail UI.
Ensure ownership-changing fields/actions are read-only/hidden for system-managed default contracts.
Update contract detail explanatory copy for system-managed ownership and assignment behavior.
Update time-entry contract banner copy from `default rates/non-contract` to `system-managed default contract` terminology.
Update usage UI messaging to mirror time-entry default-contract terminology.
Replace technical assignment labels in automatic invoicing rows with business-safe labels.
Ensure grouped-row combinability logic remains correct when rows include default-contract and explicit-contract work.
Add route-safe permission checks so only authorized users can view/edit default-contract lifecycle controls.
Prevent unauthorized API mutations of system-managed markers/identity fields.
Add structured log events for default contract ensure outcomes (created/reused).
Add structured log events for resolver routing decisions (explicit/default/ambiguous).
Add structured log events for reconciliation write-back updates and skips.
Add operational counters/trace markers for default contract created/reused and unresolved ambiguous counts.
Create schema migration(s) for any new default-contract marker/pointer fields and uniqueness constraints.
Keep migration idempotent and schema-guarded with explicit existence checks.
Implement lazy backfill strategy: no forced full historical materialization; create/link on first qualifying interaction.
Implement targeted reconciliation routine for existing null contract_line_id records with deterministic single match.
Document and enforce execution sequencing to avoid partial-cutover invalid states.
Remove dead/obsolete fallback branches after cutover verification to reduce complexity debt.
Update developer docs/runbooks describing default contract lifecycle and routing semantics.
Update API schema contracts if default-contract metadata is exposed in billing settings/contract endpoints.
Add server-side validation to prevent multiple conflicting default-contract assignments per client.
Maintain multi-active-contract support while preserving deterministic resolver precedence for default contract only as fallback.
Ensure invoice history and recurring history attribution fields display stable default-contract context.
Audit integration-created clients and ensure first billing-config touchpoint still triggers default-contract creation.
Ensure cross-package shared helpers and package action layers do not diverge in default-contract behavior.
Add cleanup guardrails so deleting billing configuration/client cannot leave dangling references to default contract pointers.
Define and enforce canonical naming convention for system-created default contracts.
Validate no regression in contract cadence and service-period invoicing flows when default-contract routing is enabled.
